settings.data_tag_desc:
Export and import tag/group data as JSON or CSV.
[Export] Write all tags and groups to a JSON file.
[Merge] Add only new tags/groups from the file (existing records are skipped).
[Upsert] Add new records and also overwrite existing ones with the same name.
settings.data_tag_export_tip: Write tag and group data to a JSON file
settings.data_tag_merge_tip: Add only new tags/groups — existing records are skipped
settings.data_tag_upsert_tip: Add new records and overwrite existing ones with the same name
settings.data_backup_desc: To back up, copy the entire data folder. To restore, put the copied data folder back. index.db and suggestions.db are caches and will be rebuilt automatically on startup if missing.
settings.data_hist_desc:
Create and restore encrypted backups of the PromptMosaic database and history thumbnails.
[Backup] Creates backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.pmbak in the selected location.
[Restore] Completely replaces the current database with the backup contents. Image files copied from Invoke are not included.
settings.data_hist_export_tip: Create an encrypted backup of the PromptMosaic database and history thumbnails
settings.data_hist_restore_tip: Completely replace current data with a backup
settings.data_hist_undo_restore_tip: Return to the state just before the last restore
settings.data_hist_restore_warn_msg:
The backup contents will completely replace the current database.
The current database will be deleted and the app state will become the backup state. Back up first if you need the current state.
Image files copied from Invoke will not be changed.

Continue?
settings.data_hist_undo_restore_none: There is no pre-restore backup to return to.
settings.data_hist_undo_restore_warn_msg:
Return to the state just before the last restore.
The current state will be saved as the next undo target.

Continue?
settings.data_hist_password_desc: Backups are encrypted so cloud storage does not see plain database or thumbnail files. The save option is a convenience for this PC and is not protection from people who can access this PC.
settings.data_hist_password_mismatch: The confirmation password does not match.
settings.data_cache_desc: index.db and suggestions.db are caches. Deleting or rebuilding them will not lose your source data.

invoke_setup.title: InvokeAI Data Fetch
invoke_setup.heading: InvokeAI Data Fetch
invoke_setup.intro:
PromptMosaic sends image-generation commands to InvokeAI
based on InvokeAI's own settings.
So it needs to fetch information from InvokeAI.

Main settings captured/used as a template:
  *Model / *Prompt / *LoRA / *Scheduler / *Steps / *CFG / *Size / *Seed
  VAE / Text encoder / Refiner / other advanced settings (VAE precision, CFG rescale, seamless, color correction, etc.)

* items can be changed in PromptMosaic at generation time.
invoke_setup.step1_title: Step 1: Start InvokeAI and fetch models/LoRA
invoke_setup.step1_detected: InvokeAI is running. Fetching model/LoRA info.
invoke_setup.step1_done: Model/LoRA info fetched. You can proceed to Step 2.
invoke_setup.step2_title: Step 2: Generate with each model to fetch templates
invoke_setup.step2_info:
Generate an image with each installed model in InvokeAI.
Then press the Fetch button on that base model's row to fetch the template.

Different VAE / refiner / text-encoder settings can be registered as separate templates.
Change those settings in InvokeAI, generate an image, then fetch.
Each fetch is registered as a new template, distinguished by name (delete ones you don't need).
invoke_setup.step2_lora_note:
⚠ The job you fetch MUST use at least one LoRA.
PromptMosaic injects LoRA by using the template's LoRA path as a sample, so it cannot fetch from a job without a LoRA.
invoke_setup.name_label: Template name for {base} (OK to keep as-is):
invoke_setup.fetch_dup_msg: That template has already been fetched.
invoke_setup.no_template_msg:
No {base} template found.
